name: Van Cliburn
abstract: Harvey Lavan "Van" Cliburn Jr. (July 12, 1934 – February 27, 2013) was an American pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Doug Carn
about_quotes.evidence: But if you go outside the established perimeters that they’ve got for you then you’ve either sold out or changed you know. They want to see you play until you die on the stage you know. You know ‘til you’re in a wheelchair and ragged and can hardly play you know. And I remember I was in Kansas City
about_quotes.doc_title: Doug Carn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Robert Harris, who is a teacher of the person in question, but does not claim any direct personal interaction with Van Cliburn.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Van Cliburn should have been marveling at that guy’s playing during that time, and he won – what? – the Tchaikovsky competition back there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I’m sitting with Andre Watts. I’m sitting with Van Cliburn. People like that are on the music panel.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
